ptxas_standalone_sm_90a.note: PTX scaffold assembles cleanly when target is sm_90a (Hopper).

cumoduleloaddataex_sm_120.interpretation: Driver-JIT (CUDA 13.0 ptxas) explicitly rejects the entire wgmma instruction class when lowering to sm_120a (Blackwell consumer / GB202).

falsifier_status: ARCH-REJECTED (not a code bug; instruction class is sm_90a-only)

architectural_finding.sm_120_tensor_core_path: Blackwell consumer (GB202 / sm_120a) does NOT inherit wgmma. It also does NOT support tcgen05 (which is sm_100a / B200 / datacenter Blackwell). Consumer Blackwell falls back to mma.sync (warp-level synchronous, Ampere-style).
architectural_finding.implication_for_cublas_gap: The 16% gap (0.84 ratio) on RTX 5070 vs cuBLAS HGEMM CANNOT be closed by transitioning from mma.sync to wgmma.async on this silicon. cuBLAS on sm_120 also uses warp-level mma.sync (matches N104 SASS-diff finding: cuBLAS picks cutlass_80_tensorop_s16816gemm = Ampere/Hopper mma.sync kernel, not a Hopper-specific wgmma kernel).
architectural_finding.where_wgmma_would_pay: H100 / H200 (sm_90a) — the original wgmma target. A Hopper-class pool host would be needed to actually fire this scaffold; on consumer Blackwell the instruction class is architecturally unavailable.

next_steps_g3: Do NOT pursue wgmma path on RTX 5070 — architecturally rejected.
Stay on mma.sync ceiling investigation for the 0.84->1.0 gap on sm_120.
If a Hopper pool host (sm_90a) becomes available, this scaffold is ready to fire there: standalone ptxas already compiled it clean (34 reg, 2560 B smem, 0 spills).
Consumer Blackwell (sm_120) gap closure must come from: (a) larger CTA tiles, (b) better mma.sync scheduling, (c) cp.async-bulk + TMA emulation if available on sm_120, NOT wgmma.
